step_by_step_thinking:
Hard swelling at the angle of mandible with numerous draining sinuses strongly points towards Ludwig's angina (Option B). This condition characterizes itself through extensive cellulitis involving the floor of the mouth and neck compartment areas like sublingual, submental, and submandibular spaces, often originating from infected third molars. It leads to signs such as induration, elevation of the floor of the mouth, trismus, difficulty swallowing, and drooling - all indicative features matching our scenario.
\boxed{B} Based on the detailed description indicating hard swelling at the angle of mandible accompanied by numerous draining sinuses, Ludwig's angina stands out given its characteristic involvement of the submandibular area and floor of the mouth, making Option B the correct identification.
